page: Amazon Web Services vt1.24xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. It features 96 dedicated virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz and supported by a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The vt1.24xlarge exhibits weak single-core performance in stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression, placing in the bottom 25%. However, its multi-core capabilities are strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU score, and Passmark CPU extended instructions. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read operations scoring in the top 25%. Additionally, the server demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, while LLM text generation speeds are poor, falling into the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the vt1.24xlarge offers high compute density with its 96 dedicated vCPUs, making it suitable for highly parallelized workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The lack of local storage means workloads must rely on network-attached storage, which is supported by the 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Given its strong multi-core processing, high memory bandwidth, and excellent web serving throughput, this instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, Redis caching layers, and parallelized CPU-bound tasks such as video transcoding or software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services vt1.24xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused server powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 25 Gbps but includes no local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks reveal a significant divergence: while single-core execution and LLM text generation are weak, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations rank in the top 25%. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads. It is best suited for high-throughput web serving, caching databases, and multi-threaded CPU tasks that leverage its high core count and memory bandwidth over network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services vt1.24xlarge is an x86_64 server with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for highly parallelized multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services vt1.24xlarge features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network speed, delivering strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core-count x86_64 compute platform delivering strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth for parallelized network-dependent workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB DDR4 RAM
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Optimized for multi-threaded applications and high-throughput web serving
